content: Laravel 11.x official docs (eloquent-mutators): backed enum casting uses protected function casts(): array returning ['status' => EnumClass::class]; attribute is automatically cast to/from the enum instance. Nullable datetime attributes use the datetime (or immutable_datetime) cast in the same casts() array; null values are never cast (stay null). Derived/computed accessors use protected function label(): Attribute { return Attribute::make(get: fn (mixed $value, array $attributes) => ...); } reading other $attributes to build a composite value — exactly the shape needed for a year-semester-count section label. Matches the casts()-method convention already used in this project's Exam/Attempt models (v1).
